articles.title: How Do You Promote Strickland vs Chimaev 2?
articles.description: How Do You Promote Strickland vs Chimaev 2? Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev may still be heading toward a rematch… but according to Chael Sonnen, there’s one massive problem: How do you promote it now? After the two fighters publicly buried the hatchet, Chael explains why the UFC suddenly lost the most valuable thing in fight promotion — personal grudge. And in combat sports, nothing sells better. Chael pulls back the curtain on how fight promotion actually works, from press conferences and weigh-ins to the hidden business behind the UFC’s biggest events. He explains why these events aren’t just promotional tools — they’re monetized content products that have to perform. So what happens when two fighters no longer hate each other? Using Ronda Rousey vs. Gina Carano as the perfect modern example, Chael explains how smart promoters create entirely new storylines when the original angle disappears. From fake company rivalries to manufactured controversy, the fight business has always been about finding a narrative people care about. That doesn’t mean the rivalry has to be fake. articles.title: Francis Ngannou on Philipe Lins, leaving UFC, Anthony Joshua fight, losing his son | Daniel Cormier
articles.description: Daniel Cormier IS BACK with the DC Show, this time the champ sits down with MMA superstar Francis Ngannou ahead of his fight against Philipe Lins on Saturday's Netflix MVP card. DC talks to the former UFC heavyweight champion about his CRAZY JOURNEY from Cameroon to France as a young man, and what it took to get to Paris 14 months later. Ngannou talks about his upbringing in Cameroon, and why he had to work in sand mines at just nine years old. DC and Francis go through his MMA journey and how transitioned from boxing into MMA and then eventually become a UFC champion. Francis also talks about the tragedy of losing his son Kobe, and how both DC and Francis have dealt with losing children. And don't miss Cormier and Ngannou talk about his Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua boxing fights, a future head-to-head with Jon Jones, and the reason why he decided to leave the UFC for the PFL. articles.title: Chimaev Opens as Favorite Over Strickland
articles.description: The betting odds are already out for a hypothetical rematch between Khamzat Chimaev and Sean Strickland… and Chimaev opened as the favorite. Chael Sonnen breaks down why that matters far more than people realize. After one of the closest fights in recent memory, Chael explains why the UFC suddenly finds itself backed into a corner. Sean Strickland now has two possible opponents Khamzat Chimaev or Nassourdine Imavov, and according to Chael, neither option is ideal. Because both are rematches. Chael reacts to the public perception surrounding the first Strickland vs. Chimaev fight, why the live crowd believed Sean won, and why Dana White’s reaction after the event may tell the bigger story. Then the conversation shifts to strategy. If Sean really does have a choice, which matchup gives him the best chance to stay champion? Which fight pays more? Which fight is actually easier? Chael explains why that’s exactly how champions are supposed to think — and why Sean needs to decide quickly before the UFC decides for him. There’s also a bigger issue quietly developing behind the scenes: the UFC’s betting partner is already leaning one direction. And once the odds hit the board… the story may already be written. #UFC #MMA #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #ChaelSonnen #Imavov #UFCNews 0:00 Chimaev Opens as the Favorite1:35 Did Sean Actually Win?3:45 Why Sean’s Options Are Both Bad6:10 The Problem With Rematches8:25 Why Sean Needs to Decide Fast10:40 The UFC May Already Be Leaning Toward Chimaev Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Strickland’s Strategy Nearly Broke Chimaev #ufc
articles.description: Sean Strickland may have delivered the smartest tactical performance of his career against Khamzat Chimaev. In this clip, the fight gets broken down round by round—and the turning point came early. The opening round looked exactly how people expected: Chimaev pushing wrestling pressure and dictating the pace. But once Strickland stuffed a key shot in the second round, the entire fight shifted. The adjustment was subtle but brilliant. By constantly backing up while staying active with the jab, Strickland limited Chimaev’s ability to explode into takedowns. Most fans saw retreating. What was really happening was tactical defense. Every step backward removed the wrestling entries Chimaev was waiting for. And Chimaev kept waiting. Throughout the championship rounds, he was hunting for one mistake—one reckless charge forward, one greedy exchange, one moment where Strickland planted his feet for the knockout. It never came. Strickland stayed disciplined for five rounds and forced one of the closest fights of Chimaev’s career. That’s what made this performance so shocking. An undefeated aura suddenly looked vulnerable. articles.title: Chael Predicts Disaster for Ronda vs Gina
articles.description: Ronda Rousey vs. Gina Carano sounds massive on paper… but Chael Sonnen sees some serious warning signs. After speaking with Jake Paul about crossover fights, boxing, and the future of combat sports, Chael shifts his focus to one of the most talked-about fantasy matchups in MMA history: Ronda vs. Gina. And according to Chael, the fight may already have one major problem. Both women look incredible physically. Ronda looks lean, muscular, and explosive. Gina looks athletic, strong, and naturally bigger. But Chael explains why appearances can be deceiving — especially in comeback fights involving former stars long removed from competition. Using examples like Chuck Liddell vs. Tito Ortiz and Mike Tyson vs. Jake Paul, Chael breaks down why aging fighters can “look ready” while still being miles away from real fight shape. Then comes the bigger issue: the fight being scheduled for five rounds. Chael explains why that decision makes no sense, why shorter fights would actually increase excitement, and why boxing negotiates almost everything except the one thing that matters most — rounds. He also revisits the original stylistic matchup between prime Gina and prime Ronda, including the size difference, takedown dynamics, and the one weakness Ronda herself openly admitted years ago. And before it’s over… Chael makes one very bold prediction. #UFC #MMA #RondaRousey #GinaCarano #ChaelSonnen #JakePaul #WomenMMA 0:00 Chael Talks With Jake Paul 1:15 Ronda and Gina Look Incredible 3:05 The Chuck vs Tito Warning Sign 5:20 Why Five Rounds Is a Huge Mistake 7:45 Prime Gina vs Prime Ronda 10:15 Chael’s Biggest Prediction for the Fight Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: The “46-Pound Weight Cut” Story Is Nonsense #ufc
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev cutting 46 pounds in a week? That story never made sense. In this clip, the rumors surrounding Chimaev’s conditioning and exhaustion get challenged directly. Fighters understand weight cuts better than anyone, and the idea that a professional athlete would publicly claim something that absurd simply doesn’t add up. More importantly, weight cuts alone don’t explain what people saw in the fight. The real factor may have been emotional exhaustion. From the tension leading into the matchup, to the heavy security presence, to the intensity between both fighters, the atmosphere was different. High emotion affects performance. Adrenaline dumps, anger, pressure, and stress can drain a fighter far more than people realize—and unlike weight cutting, that impact can show up live inside the cage. What makes this even more interesting is how the fight ended. Respect replaced hostility. One fighter extended his hand after everything that happened, and in many ways, that may have revealed who carried the bigger psychological burden throughout the night. This wasn’t just physical fatigue. It was emotional warfare. articles.title: The Truth About Zabit’s Retirement
articles.description: Zabit Magomedsharipov may be the greatest UFC “what if” of the modern era. Chael Sonnen looks back at one of the most unique and talented fighters the sport has ever seen, a fighter who had the skills, the creativity, the hype, and possibly even championship ability… but still walked away before ever fighting for a title. From Zabit’s electric UFC run to the rumors surrounding his retirement, Chael breaks down why fans were so captivated by him in the first place. The length. The style. The unpredictability. Few fighters ever felt as different as Zabit did inside the cage. But this conversation goes deeper than highlights. Chael reacts to Zabit’s recent comments about why he stepped away from MMA, including the belief that the UFC never intended to make him champion during the rise of dominant Russian fighters like Khabib Nurmagomedov and Islam Makhachev. And while Chael admits there may have been truth to some of those politics, he also explains why the truly great fighters never think that way. This becomes a bigger conversation about ambition, timing, opportunity, and what separates contenders from champions. Because being good enough to become champion… and wanting it badly enough… are not always the same thing. #UFC #MMA #Zabit #ZabitMagomedsharipov #ChaelSonnen #IslamMakhachev #Khabib 0:00 The Legend of Zabit1:35 Why Fans Loved Him3:30 Why He Never Got the Title Shot5:40 The UFC Politics Around Russian Fighters7:50 Chael’s Real Issue With Zabit10:05 Why the Greats Never Think That Way12:10 Did Zabit Really Want It? articles.title: The Problem With Chimaev vs Strickland 2
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev says he wants a rematch with Sean Strickland… and now the UFC has a real problem. Chael Sonnen breaks down why this situation is far more complicated than fans realize. On one side, Chimaev has done everything right — he’s called out everyone, fought anyone, and never backed down from a challenge. On the other side, Nassourdine Imavov may have actually earned the next shot. And that’s where things get messy. Chael explains why rematches are always dangerous business in MMA, especially when the first fight didn’t exactly set the world on fire. From rankings politics to pay-per-view reality, this becomes less about who “deserves” it and more about what fight actually makes sense for the UFC. There’s also the bigger question nobody can answer yet: Is Chimaev even staying at middleweight? After weeks of talk about moving to light heavyweight, Chimaev suddenly calling out Strickland may have finally revealed the truth. Chael reacts to the callout, explains why post-fight emotions matter more than people think, and breaks down the impossible position the UFC now finds itself in. articles.title: Chimaev’s Weight Cut Story Makes NO Sense
articles.description: Khamzat Chimaev’s weight cut story has taken over the MMA world… and Chael Sonnen says the math simply doesn’t work. After reports spread claiming Chimaev lost 46 pounds in a single week, Chael breaks down why that’s not just unlikely, it’s physically impossible. From real-world weight cutting experience to the confusion between pounds and kilograms, Chael walks through where the story may have started, why fans ran with it, and what the numbers actually show. But this conversation goes way beyond a bad headline. Chael also dives into what comes next for Chimaev after the confusion surrounding his return. Is Khamzat staying at middleweight to chase Sean Strickland? Is a move to light heavyweight still on the table? And if he does move divisions, should the UFC match him against Jiří Procházka? Then there’s the fight fans have wanted for years: Bo Nickal vs. Khamzat Chimaev. Chael explains why the timing finally makes sense, why the UFC avoided the matchup before, and why the rankings no longer stand in the way. This isn’t just about weight cutting. articles.title: Do We Need a Chimaev-Strickland Rematch? #ufc
articles.description: Sean Strickland may have lost—but he didn’t lose anyone’s respect. In this clip, the aftermath of a razor-close battle with Khamzat Chimaev gets broken down. Some people believed Strickland was winning rounds with pressure, defense, and cleaner shots. Others saw Chimaev’s forward movement and aggression carrying the fight. That’s what made the result so compelling—it truly came down to interpretation. But beyond the scorecards, there was something else people noticed: respect. The handshake, the sportsmanship, even Chimaev placing the belt around Strickland afterward—it changed the energy of the moment. Instead of bitterness, the fight ended with mutual admiration. And strangely enough, that made the rematch question even harder. Do you run it back immediately because it was a split decision and incredibly competitive? Or do you move forward because the story felt complete in that moment? That’s the real debate now. Not whether the fight was close—it clearly was. The question is what comes next. Subscribe for more MMA breakdowns, bold takes, and real insight into the fight game. #SeanStrickland #KhamzatChimaev #UFC #MMA #FightTalk #MMANews #CombatSports

articles.title: Islam Ducked Ilia Topuria!
articles.description: In this Ask Uncle Chael episode, Chael covers multiple fan questions—and doesn’t hold back. He explains why Islam’s next opponent doesn’t matter unless it’s Topuria or Arman Tsarukyan, and why anything else feels like a step sideways for a champion who already has everything. But that’s just the start. Chael also answers whether trash talk really gets inside a fighter’s head, and why he believes it almost never works—except in one very specific case. He gives a brutally honest take on whether you should train your daughter in martial arts, and why the reality of self-defense isn’t what most people think it is. He breaks down the hidden consequences of fighting, why winning can actually get you in more trouble than losing, and what people don’t consider before stepping into that world. And he even dives into bigger questions about the sport itself—who deserves title shots, what actually matters in matchmaking, and how narratives get shaped. This isn’t just about one topic. It’s about how Chael sees the entire fight game. articles.title: The Real Ilia Topuria Finally Revealed
articles.description: Ilia Topuria may be the most dangerous star in the UFC right now — and after finally meeting him in person, Chael says the reality matches the reputation. Chael tells the story of first hearing about Ilia years ago during the backstage chaos with Paddy Pimblett, long before most fans even knew who he was. Back then, Ilia was just an undefeated prospect with a perfect record and a chip on his shoulder. Now he’s the centerpiece of the UFC’s rumored White House card and one of the biggest names in the sport. But what stood out most wasn’t the fame — it was the mindset. Chael breaks down Ilia’s comments about Justin Gaethje, the honesty behind how he views fighting, and why he separates competition from personal hatred. A t the same time, there’s another side to Ilia that reminds Chael of Israel Adesanya during his legendary title run: he wants specific fights for specific reasons. That includes potential matchups with Arman Tsarukyan, Paddy Pimblett, and Islam Makhachev. And according to Chael, once Ilia feels disrespected… there’s no going back.
